name: social-impact-accountability
description: Orchestrates notification of third parties (advertisers, regulators, and civil society) regarding confirmed misinformation to achieve social responsibility and blowback against malicious actors.

steps.name: identify-stakeholders
steps.prompt_template: For the following debunked claim: {{input}}, identify associated stakeholders including the hosting platform, major advertisers on that platform, and relevant regulatory bodies or fact-checking NGOs. Include Slovak/EU-specific bodies: RTVS, Rada pre mediálne služby, AFP Fact Check SK.

steps.name: legal-review
steps.prompt_template: Review the debunked claim and associated evidence. Assess whether the content may constitute a violation of Slovak law (§ 360 TZ — šírenie poplašnej správy), EU Digital Services Act Art. 34, or relevant platform Terms of Service. Summarize legal exposure for notification drafts.

steps.name: draft-alerts
steps.prompt_template: Using the verified evidence from the debunking report and the legal assessment: {{legal_assessment}}, draft professional but firm 'Misinformation Alerts' tailored for each group in {{stakeholder_list}}. Highlight the breach of social responsibility, legal exposure, and potential reputational blowback for associated brands.

steps.name: disseminate-reports
steps.prompt_template: Prepare the drafted alerts in {{drafted_alerts}} for dissemination. Format as formal PDF reports for local storage at {{output.dir}}/social-impact/{{run.id}}/. NOTE: SMTP email delivery requires SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS environment variables — delivery will be skipped if not configured.
